description: Mechanical/judgment boundary manifest for the v3 .ai/ scaffold. Every path listed under 'mechanical' has a corresponding static template file in this directory. Paths listed under 'judgment_laden' are produced in-harness by the ai-catapult plugin and are NOT templated here. This manifest is consumed by Slice 3 parity fixtures.

classification_rules.mechanical: Path layout is deterministic and repo-agnostic; content is fixed or contains only well-typed placeholder tokens ({{REPO_ID}}, {{DATE}}, {{UPSTREAM_URL}}, {{UPSTREAM_REF}}). No domain knowledge or architecture judgment required.
classification_rules.judgment_laden: Content requires interpretation of the specific repo's domain, architecture decisions, cascade topology, or AI-failure-mode analysis. Generated in-harness by the plugin after discovery.

paths.rationale: Template emits the STANDALONE matrix skeleton only (topology_type hardcoded to 'standalone', max_allowed_depth/current_depth fixed at 0, managed_repositories empty). The umbrella matrix — which requires populated managed_repositories and max_allowed_depth=3 — is judgment-laden and plugin-generated, consistent with cascade-plan.json classification.
Role-specific instructions are fixed conventions, not repo-specific decisions.
Role-specific instructions are fixed conventions.
Role-specific instructions are fixed conventions.
Tool injection descriptor; operations list and confirmation gates are fixed.
Tool injection descriptor; fixed operations set.
Workflow phase sequence is fixed and deterministic.
Machine-readable workflow manifest; workflow_id and phase list are fixed.
Phase schema is fixed (schema_version, workflow_id, phase_id, title, required, status scaffold). Initial status is 'pending'.
Phase schema is fixed.
Phase schema is fixed.
Phase schema is fixed.

Provider-neutral tier table (ADR-0003) is fixed. host_aliases are illustrative examples included as an optional deployment convenience; teams may replace or extend them — replacing host aliases requires no architecture judgment about the repo itself.
MCP registry stub (ADR-0005) is fixed; all servers carry status:stub and no live endpoint.
A2A cross-agent handoff convention (ADR-0005) is fixed prose, not repo-specific.
Empty array at scaffold time; shape is fixed.
Baseline security guardrails are fixed conventions applicable to any repo.
Baseline technical-bounds are fixed placeholder rules; teams extend post-scaffold.
Empty/null initial drift record; shape is fixed.
Logging and trace conventions (ADR-0005) are fixed scaffold prose.
Token-cost and trajectory-audit checklist (ADR-0005) is fixed.
AI-failure-mode review checklist (spec §4.B) is fixed; failure mode categories are invariant.
Skeleton with Harness Map section and placeholder tokens is fixed; actual workflow decisions (ADRs, cascade list) are judgment-laden and filled in-harness.
Thin pointer to AGENTS.md (ADR-0004). No content-bearing sections.
Thin pointer to AGENTS.md (ADR-0004). No content-bearing sections.
Five-domain Archgate rules file with standard placeholder rules. Teams customize rule entries post-scaffold.
Hook configuration is fixed schema (validate-rules local hook).
CI workflow for prek is fixed (j178/prek-action@v2 with --all-files).
Handoff document summarises what the plugin discovered and decided about the specific repo. Generated in-harness after all four phases complete.
Traceability graph nodes reference actual BRD/PRD/issue IDs specific to the repo.
Human-readable index of traceability links; content is per-repo.
Validation report reflects discovered coverage gaps for this specific repo.
ADR body captures the specific architectural decision context of this repo. Cannot be templated without losing meaning.
Cascade plan lists specific managed repositories and host adapters discovered during phase 1. Per-repo topology judgment.
Terminal-priority human-override content is never generated; only written by humans.
Terminal-priority human-override content. Same rationale.
Written only by local agents at runtime, not scaffolded.
Written only by local agents at runtime, not scaffolded.

paths.install_destination: scripts/graph-refresh.sh — committed to the repo. ai-catapult init stages the template under graph-automation/; ai-catapult graph-hooks install activates it by stamping {{ENGINE}} and writing to scripts/graph-refresh.sh (stage-then-activate).
paths.rationale: Lock+coalesce wrapper for background graph engine refresh. Body is fixed; only the {{ENGINE}} token is substituted at install time (default: graphify). Engine-absent no-op, exits 0 always.

paths.install_destination: .git/hooks/post-commit and .git/hooks/post-checkout — git metadata only, never committed to the repo. The template staging path (graph-automation/hook-body.sh) is the source slot; the Slice-5 installer reads from there and writes to .git/hooks/.
paths.rationale: Git hook body (post-commit/post-checkout). <=15 active lines; rebase/merge/cherry-pick skip guards verbatim from graphify hooks.py:51-55; resolve-then-delegate to wrapper. Never tracked in child repos.
Harness hook config snippets for Claude Code Stop+SessionStart and Codex post_response+pre_session events. Placeholders fine; merged into .claude/settings.json and .codex/hooks.json at install time.
Engine knob: engine=graphify default, graphwiki as documented alternative. Fixed JSON shape; teams update engine value then re-run install.
Additive matrix binding schema; fixed and repo-agnostic.
Execution profile policy schema; fixed and repo-agnostic.
Sanitized child binding projection schema; fixed and repo-agnostic.

notes: Dirs without a named file get a .gitkeep in the template tree so the directory is tracked by git.

Stage-then-activate convention (graph-automation entries): ai-catapult init stages all four graph-automation templates under graph-automation/ in the target repo. ai-catapult graph-hooks install then activates them: the wrapper is stamped with {{ENGINE}} and written to scripts/graph-refresh.sh; hook bodies are written to .git/hooks/post-commit and .git/hooks/post-checkout. The path field in each entry therefore reflects the init-emit (staging) location; install_destination records the activation target.
The .ai/evals/<set>/ evalset files (evalset.json, rubric.md, judge-config.json) are judgment_laden — the eval set name and rubric content are per-repo.
The .ai/drift/backups/ dir is runtime-only (written by sync path), not scaffolded.
The .ai/skills/catalog-audit.json, description-exceptions.json, and body-line-exceptions.json are generated by the skill-catalog branch, not the base scaffold.
upstream.lock is omitted: it requires resolving a live git SHA at scaffold time and is written by foundation.md artifacts, not the .ai/ tree.
